title: ricinocarpus unibracteatus
intro: Ricinocarpus unibracteatus, also known as the single-bracted ricinocarpus, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties. Ricinocarpus unibracteatus can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used in topical applications for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
